response:
To find the characteristic polynomial of the matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar and $I$ is the identity matrix. The characteristic polynomial is given by $p(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.

Let the matrix $A$ be:
$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
 12 & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
 -13 & -7 & 13 & 2 \\
 -3 & -3 & 13 & -14 \\
 -13 & 14 & 3 & -10
\end{pmatrix}
$$

The matrix $A - \lambda I$ is:
$$
A - \lambda I = \begin{pmatrix}
 12-\lambda & -1 & -12 & 9 \\
 -13 & -7-\lambda & 13 & 2 \\
 -3 & -3 & 13-\lambda & -14 \\
 -13 & 14 & 3 & -10-\lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Calculating the determinant of a $4 \times 4$ matrix directly is computationally intensive. A more efficient approach is to calculate the coefficients of the characteristic polynomial $p(\lambda) = \lambda^4 + c_3 \lambda^3 + c_2 \lambda^2 + c_1 \lambda + c_0$.
The coefficients are related to the traces of the powers of the matrix and the sums of principal minors:
1.  $c_3 = -\text{tr}(A)$
2.  $c_2 = \frac{1}{2} [ (\text{tr}(A))^2 - \text{tr}(A^2) ]$
3.  $c_1 = -\frac{1}{6} [ (\text{tr}(A))^3 - 3\text{tr}(A)\text{tr}(A^2) + 2\text{tr}(A^3) ]$
